revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:石部神社 (豊岡市)]] {{infobox religious building |name = Isobe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Isobe-jinja (Toyooka) haiden.JPG |location = Izushi-cho Shimo, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture 62 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.461194|N|134.882028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined = Ame-no-Hikata-Kushihikata-no-Mikoto |shrine_Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] |established = Unknown |main_building = Two-bay nagare-zukuri |festival = Mid-October |map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=40.27|lon_deg=134|lon_min=52|55.28|label=Isobe Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=}} |location = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}} |deity = {{ill|Kamo no Kimi|qid=Q55809532|1=Kamo no Okimi}} }} [[File:Isobe-jinja (Toyooka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] '''Isobe Shrine''' (石部神社, ''Isobe Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|qid=Q696388|lt=Toyooka|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, Hyōgo Prefecture, in the Izushi-cho Shimo district. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and held the Rank of [[Gōsha]] under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following eight deitiesShrine history., collectively referred to as the "Eight Pillar Deities"{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}: '''Main Enshrined deity''' * '''Ame-no-Hikata-Kushihikata-no-Mikoto''' - Ancestor deity of the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588086}}. '''Additional Deities''' * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Takitsuhiko-no-Mikoto The shrine was traditionally associated with the deity {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天之日矛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}} in earlier periods{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. == History == The shrine’s founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally located in Tsuboi Village (present-day Izushi-cho Tsuboi, Toyooka City). It was relocated to a hill in Bishamon-cho in 1595 and then to its current location in 1705{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. The Tsuboi region is believed to have been a site of early development{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} of 927 as "Isobe Shrine" in the list of shrines in {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province|1=Tajima Province}}, Izushi District, and designated as a Shikinaisha{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was patronized by the {{ill|Koide clan|ja|小出氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide clan|1=Koide clan (Negi Daibu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11459112}} and {{ill|Sengoku clan (Izushi)|ja|仙石家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku clan (Izushi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仙石氏|1=Sengoku clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378184}}, the lords of {{ill|Izushi Domain|en|Izushi Domain|qid=Q10863249|lt=Izushi Domain|1=Izushi Domain}}{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in 1873 and later promoted to Gōsha status in 1879{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. == Grounds == [[File:Isobe-jinja06s4592.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Great Zelkova Tree
Designated Natural Monument of Toyooka}}]] * '''Main Hall''' – Built in 1935. A two-bay nagare-zukuri structure with a copper roof{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. * '''Worship Hall''' – Built in 1935. {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style with a tiled roof{{Sfn|Isobe Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1984}}. * '''Great Zelkova Tree''' – Estimated to be 1,000 years old, measuring 8 meters in circumference and 30 meters in height. Designated a Natural Monument of Toyooka CityOn-site explanation board.. File:Isobe-jinja (Toyooka) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Isobe-jinja02s4592.jpg|Shrine Grounds
{{small|Main shrine on the right, Imperial Shrine on the left.}} == Sub-Shrines == [[File:Isobe-jinja03st3200.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Imperial Shrine (left) and Tenman Shrine (right)}}]] * '''Imperial Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}, and Ōta-no-Mikoto (additional enshrined deities include {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56344982}}). * '''Tenman Shrine''' – Enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} and others. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the following annual festivals: * '''New Year’s Festival''' (January 1) * '''Foundation Festival''' (January 3) * '''Setsubun Festival''' (February 3) – Includes a vigil ritual. * '''Niiname-sai''' (February 17) * '''Summer Purification Festival''' (Last Sunday in June) * '''Grand Festival''' (Sunday before October 15) – Previously held on September 12 and later October 15, the festival includes a "Danji Matsuri" featuring around 20 {{ill|Danjiri|ja|だんじり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11271630}} floats clashing in a "fighting festival" at Izushi Castle{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.toyooka.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/003/884/01.pdf|title=Toyooka City Council Newsletter No. 58 (2016)|publisher=Toyooka City Council|date=November 25, 2016|accessdate=September 30, 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.toyooka.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/002/556/1101.pdf|title=Toyooka Public Relations 2015: Izushi Autumn Festival Danjiri|accessdate=September 30, 2020}}. * '''Thanksgiving Festival''' (November 23) == Cultural Properties == === Toyooka City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Natural Monuments''' ''' '''Great Zelkova Tree at Isobe Shrine''' – Designated on September 16, 1993{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.toyooka.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/001/409/shitei-shi.pdf|title=Toyooka City Cultural Properties List (No. 235)|publisher=Toyooka City|date=March 19, 2019|accessdate=September 30, 2020}}. == location == '''Address:''' * 62 Shimo, Izushi-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan. '''Access:''' * Approximately a 12-minute walk east from {{ill|Shinkorou|ja|辰鼓楼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkorou|1=Shinkorō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11637916}} in central Izushi. '''Nearby:''' [[File:Izushi-Chausuyama-kofun.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Chausuyama Kofun}}]] * '''Chausuyama Kofun''' – Designated as a Toyooka City Historic Site{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.toyooka.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/001/409/shitei-shi.pdf|title=Toyooka City Cultural Properties List (No. 196)|publisher=Toyooka City|date=March 19, 2019|accessdate=October 1, 2020}}. == References == {{Citation help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shrine history. * On-site explanation board. * {{Cite book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=Kuwabara Kimitoku|chapter=Isobe Shrine|year=1984|title=Shikinaisha Research Report Vol. 19|publisher=Kogakkan University Press}} * {{Cite book|year=1999|chapter=Urayamamachi|title=Historical Place names of Hyōgo Prefecture, Volume 1|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=978-4582490619}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Isobe-jinja (Toyooka)}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6323003.html Isobe Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Association. {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|石部神社 (豊岡市)|version=100668778|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石部神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:石部神社]] {{Commons category|Isobe-jinja (Toyooka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11587232}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Izushi Domain]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
